# Architecture Evidence Pack
<!-- Generated by Genesis Architect evidence_pack.py -->
<!-- Generated: 2026-05-19T21:55:29.370255+00:00 -->

## Research Summary

**Research quality:** THIN
**Repos scanned:** 5
**Deep analyzed:** 0

**Executive Summary:**

The Python CLI ecosystem has converged on two dominant patterns: Click for decorator-based
command composition, and Typer for type-annotated CLIs that auto-generate help text. For
single-purpose tools, Click is the clear choice with 17k stars and active maintenance.
The top projects share a consistent structural pattern: thin entry point, isolated core
logic, and explicit argument validation before any processing begins.

## Issues Mined

- https://github.com/pallets/click/issues/2416
- https://github.com/pallets/click/issues/2558
- https://github.com/pallets/click/issues/1846
- https://github.com/fastapi/typer/issues/522

## Recurring Failure Patterns

- **Business logic inside the Click callback**: Developers write transformation and validation logic directly inside
`@click.command()` decorated functions, making the logic impossible to unit test
without spawning a subprocess and capturing stdout.
- **Type annotation conflicts break mypy in Click 8.1+**: Click's internal type stubs changed in 8.1.4, causing mypy failures
on `@click.option()` decorators that previously passed type checking silently.
- **Path traversal when reading user-supplied file paths**: Accepting a raw file path from CLI args and passing it directly to
`open()` allows `../../../etc/passwd`-style traversal outside the intended working directory.
- **No input validation causes cryptic tracebacks as error messages**: Passing invalid input directly to processing functions produces Python
tracebacks visible to end users instead of clean error messages.

## Selected Architecture Decision

Chose Minimalist tier with Click based on 4 of 5 analyzed repos using decorator-based
argument parsing for single-purpose tools. The core isolation pattern (all logic in
core.py, no Click imports there) appears in all top repos and is required for testability
without subprocess spawning. tqdm integrated via context manager for progress output
on long-running operations.

## Risks Found and Mitigations Added

| # | Risk | Issue | Mitigation File | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| pitfall_1 | Business logic inside the Click callback | [link](https://github.com/pallets/click/issues/2416) | `src/python_cli/core.py` | MAPPED |
| pitfall_2 | Type annotation conflicts break mypy in Click 8.1+ | [link](https://github.com/pallets/click/issues/2558) | `NOT MAPPED` | MISSING |
| pitfall_3 | Path traversal when reading user-supplied file paths | [link](https://github.com/pallets/click/issues/1846) | `src/python_cli/utils/security.py` | MAPPED |
| pitfall_4 | No input validation causes cryptic tracebacks as error messages | [link](https://github.com/fastapi/typer/issues/522) | `src/python_cli/cli.py` | MAPPED |

## Validations Required

Run before every commit:

```bash
python scripts/genesis_subcommands.py validate .
```

This checks that every `mitigation_file_path` still exists in the project tree.
Exit 1 blocks the commit. Fix the mitigation before proceeding.
